data.content:
Here are the MSBuild evaluation performance issues in this project:

---

### 1. Deep Nested Import Chain
`EvalHeavy.csproj` → `level1.props` → `level2.props` → `level3.props`

Each `<Import>` is resolved during the **evaluation phase** — MSBuild must locate, parse, and evaluate every file before any target runs. A 3-level chain here is manageable, but this pattern scales poorly. Each level adds file I/O and XML parsing overhead. Flattening or consolidating these into fewer files reduces evaluation time.

### 2. Overly Broad Glob Pattern
```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.*" Exclude="**\bin\**;**\obj\**" />
```
The `**\*.*` glob scans the **entire directory tree** during evaluation. It will crawl into `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `packages/`, and any other directory that isn't `bin/` or `obj/`. In large repos this causes massive filesystem enumeration at evaluation time.

**Fix:** Restrict the glob to specific extensions and directories, or add exclusions via `DefaultItemExcludes`:
```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.cs;**\*.json" />
<!-- or -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <DefaultItemExcludes>$(DefaultItemExcludes);**\node_modules\**;**\.git\**</DefaultItemExcludes>
</PropertyGroup>
```

### 3. File I/O via Property Function During Evaluation
```xml
<BuildNotes Condition="Exists('build-notes.txt')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('build-notes.txt'))</BuildNotes>
```
This calls `System.IO.File.ReadAllText()` **during evaluation**, not during build execution. Property functions run inline as MSBuild evaluates properties, so every project load (including IDE open, restore, and design-time builds) pays this disk I/O cost. This should be moved to a target that runs at execution time:
```xml
<Target Name="ReadBuildNotes" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <ReadLinesFromFile File="build-notes.txt" Condition="Exists('build-notes.txt')">
    <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="_BuildNoteLines" />
  </ReadLinesFromFile>
</Target>
```

### Evaluation vs. Execution Phase
The key distinction: **evaluation** happens every time MSBuild loads the project (IDE, restore, build, design-time). It resolves imports, expands globs, and evaluates properties/conditions. **Execution** only runs during an actual build. All three issues above put expensive work in the evaluation phase, meaning they impact every single project load — not just builds.
